intro.text: Elara woke slowly, the scent of velvet and night-blooming roses filling her senses. She was not in a cold prison cell.
Instead, she lay in an elegant four-poster bed draped in black lace and deep crimson silk. Moonlight filtered through stained-glass windows patterned with thorny roses.
This was clearly someone’s personal chamber — luxurious, gothic, and unmistakably a vampire’s.
Her hand instinctively went to her neck. The bite mark was still warm… and strangely sensitive.
A faint scent of roses lingers in the air.
Where… am I? This isn’t a dungeon… This is her room.

intro.speaker: Elara
intro.text: (whispering to herself, voice shaky) Where… am I? This isn’t a dungeon… This is her room.
Heart pounding, she sat up. Escape was her only thought. She scanned the dimly lit chamber.

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: Hmm? There is a diary book? I am curious what's inside... Maybe I'll take a peek.

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: These are… so beautiful. I’ve never seen dresses like this before. They look like something from a dark fairy tale…
She reached out and gently touched the fabric. It felt incredibly soft despite its dramatic appearance.
For a brief moment, she forgot the danger she was in.

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: (talking to herself, flustered) I wonder if this would even fit me… Wait—no! What am I thinking? There’s no time for this. I need to find a way out!
Still, her fingers lingered on one particularly elegant black-and-crimson dress with delicate lace trims and soft velvet accents.

dialogues.speaker: Elara
dialogues.text: The door is unlocked. The one who captured me didn't bother to keep me here... I wonder why?
